title: wrap_coap does not validate payload length vs IPv6 limits
description:
File: python/src/lichen/coap/schc_channel.py:35-51

wrap_coap creates an IPv6 packet without checking payload size:
```python
def wrap_coap(src, dst, coap, *, src_port=DEFAULT_COAP_PORT, dst_port=DEFAULT_COAP_PORT):
    udp = UdpDatagram(src_port, dst_port, coap).to_bytes(src, dst)
    header = IPv6Header(
        src_addr=src,
        dst_addr=dst,
        next_header=UDP_NEXT_HEADER,
        payload_length=len(udp),
    )
    return header.to_bytes() + udp
```

IPv6 payload_length is a 16-bit field (max 65535). If coap payload exceeds this (e.g., due to SCHC reassembly error or malicious input), the header will have an incorrect truncated length.

For LoRa/LICHEN, practical limits are much lower, but the function doesn't enforce them.

Fix: Validate len(udp) <= 65535 and raise ValueError if exceeded.
